title: EPSG Geodetic Parameter Dataset

Sys. 1989 | | [GCJ-02](/wiki/Restrictions_on_geographic_data_in_China "Restrictions on geographic data in China") | Chinese obfuscated datum 2002 | | [Geo URI](/wiki/Geo_URI_scheme "Geo URI scheme") | Internet link to a point 2010 |  * [International Terrestrial Reference System](/wiki/International_Terrestrial_Reference_System "International Terrestrial Reference System") * [Spatial Reference System Identifier (SRID)](/wiki/SRID "SRID") * [Universal Transverse Mercator (UTM)](/wiki/Universal_Transverse_Mercator_coordinate_system "Universal Transverse Mercator coordinate system")

(also **EPSG registry**) is a public registry of [geodetic datums](/wiki/Geodetic_datum "Geodetic datum"), [spatial reference systems](/wiki/Spatial_reference_system "Spatial reference system"), [Earth ellipsoids](/wiki/Earth_ellipsoid "Earth ellipsoid"), coordinate transformations and related [units of measurement](/wiki/Unit_of_measurement "Unit of measurement"), originated by a member of the [European Petroleum Survey Group](/wiki/European_Petroleum_Survey_Group "European Petroleum Survey Group") (EPSG) in 1985. Each entity is assigned an **EPSG code** between 1024 and 32767,[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) along with a standard machine-readable [well-known text (WKT) representation](/wiki/Well-known_text_representation_of_coordinate_reference_systems "Well-known text representation of coordinate reference systems"). Sys. (QZSS) (Japan)](/wiki/Quasi-Zenith_Satellite_System "Quasi-Zenith Satellite System") * [Discrete Global Grid and Geocoding](/wiki/Discrete_Global_Grid "Discrete Global Grid") |
| Standards (history)  |  |  | | --- | --- | | [NGVD 29](/wiki/Sea_Level_Datum_of_1929 "Sea Level Datum of 1929") | Sea Level Datum 1929 | | [OSGB36](/wiki/OSGB36 "OSGB36") | Ordnance Survey Great Britain 1936 | | [SK-42](/wiki/SK-42_reference_system "SK-42 reference system") | Systema Koordinat 1942 goda | | [ED50](/wiki/ED50 "ED50") | European Datum 1950 | | [SAD69](/wiki/South_American_Datum#SAD69 "South American Datum") | South American Datum 1969 | | [GRS 80](/wiki/GRS_80 "GRS 80") | Geodetic Reference System 1980 | | [ISO 6709](/wiki/ISO_6709 "ISO 6709") | Geographic point coord. 1983 | | [NAD 83](/wiki/North_American_Datum#North_American_Datum_of_1983 "North American Datum") | North American Datum 1983 | | [WGS 84](/wiki/World_Geodetic_System "World Geodetic System") | World Geodetic System 1984 | | [NAVD 88](/wiki/North_American_Vertical_Datum_of_1988 "North American Vertical Datum of 1988") | N. American Vertical Datum 1988 | | [ETRS89](/wiki/European_Terrestrial_Reference_System_1989 "European Terrestrial Reference System 1989") | European Terrestrial Ref. Sys. 1989 | | [GCJ-02](/wiki/Restrictions_on_geographic_data_in_China "Restrictions on geographic data in China") | Chinese obfuscated datum 2002 | | [Geo URI](/wiki/Geo_URI_scheme "Geo URI scheme") | Internet link to a point 2010 |  * [International Terrestrial Reference System](/wiki/International_Terrestrial_Reference_System "International Terrestrial Reference System") * [Spatial Reference System Identifier (SRID)](/wiki/SRID "SRID") * [Universal Transverse Mercator (UTM)](/wiki/Universal_Transverse_Mercator_coordinate_system "Universal Transverse Mercator coordinate system") |
| * [v](/wiki/Template:Geodesy "Template:Geodesy") * [t](/wiki/Template_talk:Geodesy "Template talk:Geodesy") * [e](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Template:Geodesy "Special:EditPage/Template:Geodesy") |

**EPSG Geodetic Parameter Dataset** (also **EPSG registry**) is a public registry of [geodetic datums](/wiki/Geodetic_datum "Geodetic datum"), [spatial reference systems](/wiki/Spatial_reference_system "Spatial reference system"), [Earth ellipsoids](/wiki/Earth_ellipsoid "Earth ellipsoid"), coordinate transformations and related [units of measurement](/wiki/Unit_of_measurement "Unit of measurement"), originated by a member of the [European Petroleum Survey Group](/wiki/European_Petroleum_Survey_Group "European Petroleum Survey Group") (EPSG) in 1985. Each entity is assigned an **EPSG code** between 1024 and 32767,[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) along with a standard machine-readable [well-known text (WKT) representation](/wiki/Well-known_text_representation_of_coordinate_reference_systems "Well-known text representation of coordinate reference systems"). The dataset is maintained by the [IOGP](/wiki/International_Association_of_Oil_%26_Gas_Producers "International Association of Oil & Gas Producers") Geomatics Committee.[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3)

Most [geographic information systems](/wiki/Geographic_information_system "Geographic information system") (GIS) and GIS [libraries](/wiki/Library_(computing) "Library (computing)") use EPSG codes as [Spatial Reference System Identifiers](/wiki/Spatial_Reference_System_Identifier "Spatial Reference System Identifier") (SRIDs) and EPSG definition data for identifying coordinate reference systems, projections, and performing transformations between these systems, while some also support SRIDs issued by other organizations (such as [Esri](/wiki/Esri "Esri")).

## Common EPSG codes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=EPSG_Geodetic_Parameter_Dataset&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Common EPSG codes")]

* EPSG:4326 - [WGS 84](/wiki/WGS_84 "WGS 84") datum ensemble for 2D (latitude, longitude) coordinates with 2 meter accuracy, used by the [Global Positioning System](/wiki/Global_Positioning_System "Global Positioning System") among others.
* EPSG:3857 - [Web Mercator projection](/wiki/Web_Mercator_projection "Web Mercator projection") of WGS 84, used for display by many web-based mapping tools, including [Google Maps](/wiki/Google_Maps "Google Maps") and [OpenStreetMap](/wiki/OpenStreetMap "OpenStreetMap").
* EPSG:9989 - [International Terrestrial Reference Frame](/wiki/International_Terrestrial_Reference_Frame "International Terrestrial Reference Frame") 2020 (ITRF2020) for 3D coordinates (latitude, longitude, ellipsoidal height) with subcentimeter accuracy, used for monitoring continental drift among others.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=EPSG_Geodetic_Parameter_Dataset&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

The dataset was created in 1985 by Jean-Patrick Girbig of [Elf](/wiki/Elf_Aquitaine "Elf Aquitaine"), to "standardize, improve and share spatial data between members of the European Petroleum Survey Group".[[5]](#cite_note-5) It was made public in 1993.[[6]](#cite_note-:1-6)

In 2005, the EPSG organisation was merged into [International Association of Oil & Gas Producers](/wiki/International_Association_of_Oil_%26_Gas_Producers "International Association of Oil & Gas Producers") (IOGP), and became the Geomatics Committee. However, the name of the EPSG registry was kept to avoid confusion.[[6]](#cite_note-:1-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7) Since then, the acronym "EPSG" became increasingly synonymous with the dataset or registry itself.[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3)

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=EPSG_Geodetic_Parameter_Dataset&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: See also")]

* [List of map projections](/wiki/List_of_map_projections "List of map projections")
